campbell.title: Campbell City Council — July 7, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council is holding public hearings on renewing an interim ordinance tied to a state Senate law and on revoking business licenses and tobacco retailer permits for two local shops, California Cigar and Hayes Smoke Shop. The council will also vote on applying for a grant to fund public works projects and on approving new consultant service agreements.
keyTopics: Renewal of interim ordinance implementing a state Senate bill
Revocation of business license and tobacco retailer permit for California Cigar
Revocation of business license and tobacco retailer permit for Hayes Smoke Shop
Public Works Director authorized to submit a grant application
Approval of new consultant service agreements

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — May 6, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council will hold a public hearing and take initial steps toward approving an annual assessment district report and a related funding measure, with a follow-up ordinance vote set for May 20. Council members will also discuss whether Saratoga should consider becoming a charter city and review early estimates of possible new local taxes and how much revenue they could raise.
keyTopics: Public hearing and preliminary approval of the annual Engineer's Report for a city assessment district
Resolution of Intention as a step toward a future ordinance, with final adoption planned for May 20
Discussion of charter city status and what it would mean for Saratoga
Review of potential new tax options and preliminary revenue estimates for the city

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council will review routine business including February's financial report and prior closed session minutes, and vote on several contracts—a construction agreement with QLM, Inc., a three-year professional services deal, and an amendment to an existing agreement. The council will also hold a court-ordered hearing on appeals and take public comment on select agenda items.
keyTopics: Construction contract award to QLM, Inc.
Resolution to prepare plans for a town improvement project
Three-year professional services agreement approval
Second amendment to an existing town agreement
Court-ordered hearing on appeals

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 23, 2026
san-jose.summary: This San José City Council meeting covered routine approvals along with a few business items, including updates to a retail concession agreement at the airport and changes to airport fees charged to airlines and other users. The Council also reviewed a request for the City Manager to travel to Tampa, Florida.
keyTopics: Approval of City Council and Council Committee meeting minutes
Amendment to the retail merchandising concession agreement with PRI-San José, LLC at the airport
Changes to airport fees and charges rates
City Manager's approved travel to Tampa, Florida

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 23, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to discuss the city's finances, water planning, and several construction projects, alongside recognizing community milestones. Council reviewed next year's budget limits and spending policies, adopted an updated 5-year water management plan, and approved the upcoming year's capital improvement program covering local infrastructure work. The council also issued proclamations honoring Immigrant Heritage Month, the Kiwanis Club's 100th anniversary, and City Attorney Jennifer Logue's appointment to a judgeship.
keyTopics: Proclamations for National Immigrant Heritage Month, Kiwanis Club's 100th anniversary, and City Attorney Jennifer Logue's judicial appointment
Closed-door legal discussion on two potential lawsuits facing the city
Setting next year's spending limit and updating the city's financial and budgetary policy
Adopting the 5-year Urban Water Management Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan
Approving the 2026-27 Capital Improvement Program, including work on the Public Safety Building, Shoreline Golf Links parking lot repaving, and the South Bay Salt Pond Restoration project

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — July 21, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: Sunnyvale's City Council held a special study session and regular meeting covering a proposal to stop using chemical pesticides on city-owned or leased property, routine approval of city bills and June meeting minutes, and two contract items: an amendment for library book supply services and a $1.39 million contract to repair levees at the Water Pollution Control Plant's oxidation ponds.
keyTopics: Proposal to eliminate chemical pesticide use on city-owned or leased property
Contract award to Brannon Corporation for Water Pollution Control Plant oxidation pond levee repairs
Amended library services agreement with Brodart Co. for books and shelf-ready materials
Routine approval of city bills/claims and June 16 meeting minutes
Special study session held ahead of the regular council meeting

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — July 21, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council met privately with its attorneys to discuss three ongoing lawsuits against the City of Cupertino. No public votes or decisions were part of this closed-door legal strategy session, which is standard procedure when a city is involved in active litigation.
keyTopics: Lawsuit: Lixin (Leon) Chen, et al. v. City of Cupertino
Lawsuit: Yes in My Backyard, et al. v. City of Cupertino
Lawsuit: Garden Gate Coalition for Mary Avenue Safety v. City of Cupertino
Closed-session legal strategy discussion with city attorneys

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — June 23, 2026
santa-clara.summary: This notice covers logistical details for the June 23, 2026 Santa Clara City Council meeting rather than specific agenda items. The meeting is being held in a hybrid format, allowing residents to attend in person or participate remotely via Zoom or phone, and written comments can be submitted in advance through the city's online eComment system.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format combining in-person and remote attendance
Zoom access details (meeting link, ID, and dial-in phone number)
Instructions for submitting written public comment via the eComment tool
Public participation procedures ahead of the Council meeting

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: Milpitas City Council met to consider changes to how federal housing and community development funds are allocated under the 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan, along with a proposed change to the rules for filling City Council vacancies that would shorten the timeline involved. Council members also reviewed a list of new project or policy ideas submitted by individual councilmembers and went over upcoming meeting schedules and agenda items.
keyTopics: Proposed amendment to the 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan affecting housing/community development funding priorities
Ordinance change to speed up or modify the process for filling a vacant Council seat
New workplan project ideas submitted by individual Councilmembers for consideration
Review of upcoming City Council meeting calendar and schedule
Preview of items planned for the next regular Council meeting

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — June 15, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council honored retiring city figure Bill Gutoff with a formal proclamation of appreciation. The bulk of the meeting was a required public hearing (under Proposition 218) where the Council considered rate increases for water, wastewater, and refuse (trash) services, following recommendations from the city's Finance Committee. These changes would affect what residents and businesses pay on their monthly utility bills.
keyTopics: Proclamation honoring Bill Gutoff's retirement
Proposition 218 public hearing on utility rate changes
Proposed water rate increases (Schedules W-1, W-2, W-3, W-4, W-7) and FY 2027 financial forecast
Proposed wastewater/sewer rate increases (Schedules S-1, S-2, S-6, S-7) and reserve fund management
Proposed refuse/trash collection rate changes (Schedules R-1, R-C)
